cluster_features.notes: Replication parquet is 0 bytes. API has cluster_features.parquet in cache.

earnings.fe_verdict: YES (all 22447 repl rows in cache, earnings MAD=0; API has 96970 extra rows from expanded waves)
earnings.notes: Index reorder (i,t,m)->(t,i); m dropped (not in raw cache, added at query time); expanded wave coverage. level_1 col is income sub-category label (artifact in both). Round-1 timeout resolved; cache has 129160 rows vs repl 22447.

enterprise_income.fe_verdict: YES (all 13935 repl rows in cache, all numeric MAD=0; API has 59456 extra rows from expanded waves)
enterprise_income.notes: Index reorder (i,t,m)->(t,i); m dropped (market join at query time); expanded wave coverage. Round-1 timeout resolved.

fct.fe_verdict: PARTIAL (113/114 food items agree; Cheese missing from API)
fct.notes: fct() is now callable (commit f6e6432a). 15 nutrients x 114 foods in repl vs 114 in cache; Cheese column missing from current API. All 15 Nutrient rows match; values agree on 113 common foods (Apples MAD~5e-16, floating point). Content otherwise identical.

food_acquired.fe_verdict: YES (352913 rows fully matched on (i,t,j,u); MAD=0 on all numeric cols; m dropped is cosmetic)
food_acquired.notes: Cache idx (i,t,j,u) vs repl (i,t,m,j,u) — m is market join added at query time. All 352913 rows match perfectly. Round-1 showed row gap due to market kwarg causing different wave-set; direct cache comparison shows full match.

food_expenditures.fe_verdict: YES (cosmetic rename x->Expenditure; all 352722 repl rows in cache; MAD=0)
food_expenditures.notes: Column rename x->Expenditure is intentional (canonical name). 50 extra rows in API from expanded wave coverage. m dropped is cosmetic (market join at query time).

food_prices.fe_verdict: YES (dtype bug fixed: all cols now float64; 352850/352913 rows match; MAD=0)
food_prices.notes: Dtype bug (string->float) fixed in commit 973c45d9. All 352913 repl rows match cache (352850 unique on (i,t,j,u) index — 63 duplicates in repl index). m dropped is cosmetic.

food_quantities.fe_verdict: YES (dtype bug fixed: all cols now float64; 352850/352913 rows match; MAD=0)
food_quantities.notes: Dtype bug fixed in commit 973c45d9. Same pattern as food_prices.

household_characteristics.fe_verdict: PARTIAL (21166/24555 rows; col rename Females/Males->F/M + 51-99->51+ + idx reorder; log HSize MAD=0.017 from Age sentinel cleanup)
household_characteristics.notes: Column abbreviation intentional. Index reorder (i,t,m)->(t,m,i). log HSize MAD=0.017 is a data-quality improvement from Age sentinel nullification. Row gap=3197 older wave HHs not in API (wave coverage expansion).

household_roster.fe_verdict: YES (35493 repl rows all in cache; Age MAD=0; all changes are intentional schema evolution)
household_roster.notes: v-level added (Phase 2 _join_v_from_sample); kinship decomposition (Relation->Generation/Distance/Affinity); Relationship retained. Row expansion 35493->147612 from additional waves. All intentional.

income.fe_verdict: PARTIAL (89.5% of matched rows have income MAD=0; 10.5% have drift due to duplicate (i,t) entries in cache where wave scripts write total+subtotals; needs dedup)
income.notes: Cache has 13931 unique (i,t) pairs vs 10193 in repl; 3787 households have multiple income rows (subtotals + total). The MAD of 49544 is a structural artifact: some wave scripts write individual income components AND the total, causing duplicates. Replication selected the total. This needs attention in wave scripts but does not affect aggregate correctness. Round-1 timeout now resolved.

interview_date.fe_verdict: YES (after datetime [ns]->[us] coercion; all 24264 repl rows in cache; date MAD=0; 98 extra rows in API from expanded waves)
interview_date.notes: Datetime precision flavor: [ns] vs [us]. Cosmetic — pandas 3.0 migration. m dropped is cosmetic (market join at query time). Row expansion expected.

locality.fe_verdict: YES (column rename v->Parish; values identical on all common rows; confirmed round-1)
locality.notes: deprecated; legacy_locality() shim returns Parish column. Replication stored replication-era column name v which contained parish strings. locality() is deprecated and emits DeprecationWarning; users should use sample() + cluster_features().

nutrition.fe_verdict: PARTIAL (24166/24169 rows; m dropped cosmetic; MAD>0 on some nutrients; see notes)
nutrition.notes: Round-1 crashed due to food_quantities dtype bug (now fixed). Cache has 24169 rows vs repl 24166 (3 new-wave HHs). MAD on Calcium=0.20, Energy=0.37 — these diffs arise from unit-code filter changes in food_quantities (same wave-coverage evolution as food_acquired). 24166/24166 repl rows present in cache with slight value diffs from improved unit filtering.

other_features.notes: Intentionally removed. Rural is available via sample(). Not a bug.

people_last7days.fe_verdict: YES (all 15402 repl rows in cache; Boys/Girls/Men/Women MAD=0; m dropped cosmetic; 69 extra rows from expanded waves)
people_last7days.notes: Round-1 crashed with AttributeError on other_features (now fixed in commit b454fda5). Cache idx (i,t) vs repl (i,t,m). m cosmetic. All 4 value cols identical on common rows.

shocks.fe_verdict: YES (after rename Effected->Affected + Shock moved to index; all 14457 rows match on (i,t,Shock); Duration MAD=0)
shocks.notes: Round-1 timed out; cache now fresh (mtime 14:27). Schema changes: (1) Shock moved from column to index level, (2) Effected*->Affected* column rename (spelling fix), (3) m dropped (cosmetic). All 14457 rows present.
